title: Decision: The coding-memory quality benchmark is now package-callable, not only a source-t
summary: Decision: The coding memory quality benchmark is now package callable, not only a source tree script. benchmarkCodingMemoryQuality lives in mcp/kernel.ts, kage benchmark memory quality exposes it through the CLI, and kag
body: Decision: The coding-memory quality benchmark is now package-callable, not only a source-tree script. benchmarkCodingMemoryQuality lives in mcp/kernel.ts, kage benchmark --memory-quality exposes it through the CLI, and kage_benchmark accepts mode=memory_quality for MCP users. The repo script benchmarks/coding-memory-quality.mjs now delegates to the packaged kernel function so npm users and source users exercise the same benchmark logic. Why: Kage should ship benchmark proof with the product package instead of relying on repo-only artifacts. Verified by: node mcp/dist/cli.js benchmark --memory-quality; node benchmarks/coding-memory-quality.mjs --out /tmp/kage-coding-memory-quality-packaged.json; npm test --prefix mcp.
